From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1990 AFC U-16 Championship

| Tournament details | |
| --- | --- |
| Host country | [United Arab Emirates](/wiki/United_Arab_Emirates "United Arab Emirates") |
| Dates | 19–29 October |
| Teams | [7](#Qualification) (from 1 confederation) |
| Final positions | |
| Champions | [Qatar](/wiki/Qatar_national_under-17_football_team "Qatar national under-17 football team") (1st title) |
| Runners-up | [United Arab Emirates](/wiki/United_Arab_Emirates_national_under-17_football_team "United Arab Emirates national under-17 football team") |
| Third place | [China](/wiki/China_national_under-17_football_team "China national under-17 football team") |
| Fourth place | [Indonesia](/wiki/Indonesia_national_under-17_football_team "Indonesia national under-17 football team") |
| Tournament statistics | |
| Matches played | 13 |
| Goals scored | 32 (2.46 per match) |
| ← [1988](/wiki/1988_AFC_U-16_Championship "1988 AFC U-16 Championship")  [1992](/wiki/1992_AFC_U-17_Championship "1992 AFC U-17 Championship") → | |

International football competition

The **1990 AFC U-16 Championship**, was the fourth iteration of the AFC U-16 Championship, a tournament organised by the [Asian Football Confederation](/wiki/Asian_Football_Confederation "Asian Football Confederation") (AFC) and held every two years for Asian under-16 teams.

[United Arab Emirates](/wiki/United_Arab_Emirates "United Arab Emirates") was the host nation. The tournament was played out in [Dubai](/wiki/Dubai "Dubai"), [Sharjah](/wiki/Sharjah "Sharjah"), and [Abu Dhabi](/wiki/Abu_Dhabi "Abu Dhabi").
